title: [lichen_l2.c:959-960] lichen_link_init called without prior lichen_link_cleanup
description:
Category not checked: Init/reinit symmetry

Bug found: In lichen_l2_iface_init() line 959:
  lichen_link_init(&link_ctx, eui64);

This is called without first calling lichen_link_cleanup(). For initial boot, link_ctx is zero-initialized (static storage), so this is fine.

But if lichen_l2_iface_init() were ever called twice (which the invariant check at lines 970-974 attempts to prevent), the second call would re-init without cleanup.

The check at lines 970-974 catches the case where lichen_iface is already set, but that's AFTER the lichen_link_init() call. So if something goes wrong between lines 959 and 970, we've already re-initialized link_ctx without cleanup.

Actually, looking more carefully: if lichen_iface != NULL at line 970, we set iface_init_failed and return. But we already called lichen_link_init() at line 959. The prior link_ctx state is lost without cleanup.

Impact: P4 - In edge case of double iface_init, link_ctx is re-initialized without cleanup (but this path shouldn't happen in practice).
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation explaining init/cleanup symmetry. The link_ctx_initialized check prevents re-init without cleanup, and the lichen_iface invariant check catches double-iface_init attempts.
